title: AutoJML: Generation and Verification of JML Specifications Using LLM Agents
data:
RS Ayon, S Ahmed- IEEE Pulse, 2026
Formal specifications such as Java Modeling Language (JML) are essential for 
program verification, but are complex and error-prone to write manually. Although 
recent large language model (LLM) based approaches automate specification 
generation, they often struggle with complex control flow and semantic 
completeness. We present AutoJML, an LLM agent that generates and refines JML 
specifications using iterative verification and mutation-based feedback. Evaluated on

title: CorrEvator: Evaluating Patch Correctness in Automated Program Repair from Online Community Discussions
data:
Y Wang, S Guo, Z Tao, H Li, J Tan, L Hao, F Zhu- IEEE Transactions on, 2026
Automatic Program Repair (APR) aims to automatically fix software bugs by 
diagnosing the root cause of failures and suggesting potential solutions, effectively 
reducing developers' debugging time and the cost and effort associated with bug 
identification and correction. However, existing works suffer from two challenges to 
efficient evaluation of the correctness of the patches: disregarding syntactic structure 
information in bug reports and patch descriptions, and lack of joint reasoning
